title: prangos colladonioides
intro: Prangos colladonioides, also known as Ferula, is a perennial plant species belonging to the carrot family (Apiaceae) and is commonly found in mountainous regions of Central Asia. Traditionally, it has been used in medicine for its medicinal properties, which include its antibacterial, anti-inflammatory, and antiseptic properties. The health benefits associated with Ferula include its ability to reduce fever, relieve pain, and treat various respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs. Ferula can be prepared into herbal teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are often used in traditional medicine to treat a range of health conditions.
